content.text:
Module:
Machine Learning  |
Semester:
2  |
Type:
UEM2
VHS:
63h  |
Weekly hours:
4h30  |
Lectures:
1h30  |
TD:
1h30  |
TP:
1h30
Personal work VHS:
42h  |
Coefficient:
2  |
Credits:
5
Teaching Objectives
Introduction to the field, supervised and unsupervised learning techniques, and advanced topics such as deep learning and natural language processing
Essential mathematical foundations (linear algebra, statistics), Python programming and practical aspects: data preprocessing, feature engineering, model selection, training, evaluation and deployment
Recommended Prerequisites
Mathematics: linear algebra, probability, statistics, etc.
Course Content (Lectures: 21h)
Introduction and Fundamentals of Machine Learning
Machine learning: definition and application domains
Learning types (supervised, unsupervised, semi-supervised, reinforcement, autonomous)
Mathematical foundations: linear algebra, statistics and probabilities, random variables and distributions, Bayesian thinking
Machine learning workflow: steps of a ML project (from data collection to deployment)
Supervised Learning
Regression: linear, polynomial, etc.
Classification: logistic regression, SVM, decision trees and ensemble methods (random forests, gradient boosting)
Model evaluation: RMSE, accuracy, precision, recall, F1 score
Unsupervised Learning
Clustering: K-means, hierarchical (agglomerative, divisive), density-based (DBSCAN, OPTICS)
Evaluation metrics: silhouette score, Davies-Bouldin index, adjusted Rand index, mutual information
Dimensionality reduction: PCA, t-SNE, LDA, ICA, UMAP, Factor Analysis
Anomaly detection: outlier identification
Association rule learning: Apriori, Eclat, FP-Growth algorithms
Deep Learning
Introduction to neural networks: basic architecture, operating principles
Deep learning architectures: CNN, RNN
Training deep learning models: TensorFlow, PyTorch
Advanced Topics
Natural Language Processing (NLP)
Time series analysis
Recommendation systems
Reinforcement learning
Assessment
Semester exam (in-person): 60%
Continuous assessment (CC): 40% — continuous tests, personal work
Bibliographic References
Andreas Müller and Sarah Guido (2016). Introduction to Machine Learning with Python. O'Reilly.
Bastien, L. (2024). Machine Learning et Big Data. https://www.lebigdata.fr/machine-learning-et-big-data
Gullitti, T. et LLC, R.B. (2017). Application Of Machine Learning Algorithms To On-Board Diagnostics (Obd Ii) Threshold Determination.
Chapelle, O., Scholkopf, B. et Zien, Eds., A. (2009). Semi-Supervised Learning. IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks, 20(3), p: 542–542.
Batta, M. (2018). Machine Learning Algorithms: A Review. https://www.researchgate.net/publication/344717762
